title: Events, Observability, Capacity, Inventory, and Cost Control · Language Lens: Inventory Storage Lens
summary: Operators have high-cardinality object-key metrics, duplicate unordered events, incomplete request traces, no inventory reconciliation, and no alert tied to a user or cost outcome. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the inventory storage lens increment in storage telemetry, inventory, capacity, and cost model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Use S3 Inventory, Storage Lens, analytics, and sampled reconciliation for object estate, encryption, replication, lifecycle, ownership, and cost evidence.
Measure admitted and completed bytes, latency, failures, cache hit, range behavior, retries, backlog, replication, restore, and spend with bounded labels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Inventory Storage Lens · Theory Inventory Storage Lens
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3618 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to handle duplicate unordered events with bounded telemetry cardinality and cost; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
